decks.name: Types of Nouns
decks.description: Learn about common nouns, proper nouns, and other types of naming words

cards.back: A noun is a naming word. It is the name of a person, place, animal, or thing. Examples: boy, school, cat, book.
A common noun is a general name for a person, place, or thing. It does not start with a capital letter. Examples: city, dog, teacher, river.
cards.hint: It is a general name, not a specific one

cards.back: A proper noun is the special name of a particular person, place, or thing. It always starts with a capital letter. Examples: India, Riya, Monday, Ganga.
cards.hint: It always begins with a capital letter

cards.back: A collective noun names a group of people, animals, or things. Examples: a flock of birds, a bunch of grapes, a team of players.
cards.hint: Think of a word for a whole group

cards.back: Yes! 'Happiness' is an abstract noun. It names a feeling or idea that you cannot see or touch. Other examples: love, kindness, bravery.
cards.hint: Can you touch or see happiness?

decks.name: Pronouns
decks.description: Understand pronouns and how they replace nouns in sentences

cards.back: A pronoun is a word used in place of a noun. Instead of saying 'Ravi is kind. Ravi helps everyone,' we say 'Ravi is kind. He helps everyone.' Here, 'He' is the pronoun.
cards.hint: It takes the place of a naming word

cards.back: Common pronouns are: I, you, he, she, it, we, they, me, him, her, us, them. Example: 'She likes to read books.'
We use pronouns so that we do not repeat the same noun again and again. They make our sentences shorter and smoother to read.
cards.hint: What happens if you say a name too many times?

cards.front: Pick the pronoun: 'Meera has a cat. She feeds it every day.'
cards.back: 'She' is a pronoun (it replaces Meera) and 'it' is a pronoun (it replaces cat). Both are pronouns used in place of nouns.
cards.hint: Which words replaced Meera and cat?

cards.front: What is the difference between 'he' and 'him'?
cards.back: 'He' is used when the person is doing the action (subject). 'Him' is used when the action is done to the person (object). Example: 'He threw the ball. I gave it to him.'
cards.hint: One does the action, the other receives it
